optimal_offer_timing.description: Offer made after positive shift moments confirmed — specifically after lead expresses frustration with current provider (GL-MGMT) or expresses urgency about their timeline (GL-TIME). Never before qualification phase completes.
optimal_offer_timing.evidence: Vanessa Wilson-Wood (036): offer after confirming Pass the Keys failures. Henry Roberts (045): offer after documenting MyProperty Host operational failures. Both converted within 10 days of offer.

green_light_combinations.notes: EX-STL leads switching from named competitor with documented failures are the highest-probability conversion pathway. When GL-MGMT is present + clear operational failure is documented, offer should be made in the same meeting.
Best combination. Lead is already in market AND has a timeline. Both Vanessa (GL-MGMT) and Helen Bias (GL-MGMT + GL-TIME) are in this group.

Self-managing leads who want to 'try it themselves first' — offer at time of GL-SELF detection has 0% acceptance. Allow them to self-manage, then re-engage at 3 months when the operational reality has hit.
Time-driven leads respond well to offer + deadline combination. Helen Bias (GL-TIME): offer before US return = high intent.

delay_present_at_offer_outcomes.description: When an offer is made despite an unresolved delay signal being present
delay_present_at_offer_outcomes.notes: Offers made against DLY-PUR or DLY-TEN signals before the blocking event resolves consistently stall. Exception: DLY-MOV where the move is imminent (within 30 days) — these can still convert if offer deadline matches move date.

offer_framings.verbatim_framing: 13% plus VAT if you sign before [specific date] — that's down from our standard 15%
offer_framings.notes: Most common framing. Works because it anchors to 15% standard and creates genuine urgency through a specific date.

offer_framings.verbatim_framing: I want to lock that in for you now — 13% matched to your [existing property] rate
offer_framings.notes: Used with Gareth Howard (044 — existing client). 100% acceptance because trust is pre-established. Rate match removes any negotiation.

loss-averse-switcher.best_framing: Frame offer as protecting against switching risk: 'Lock this in now so you don't have to worry about the rate changing.' Pair with specific date.
loss-averse-switcher.notes: Henry Roberts (045) converted. Key: address the switching risk BEFORE presenting the offer. Lead must be convinced Stayful is operationally reliable first.

analytical-evaluator.best_framing: Present the mathematical case: 'At 13% vs 15%, on your projected £X annual gross, that's £Y saved per year.' Give them the calculation, not just the percentage.
analytical-evaluator.notes: Isobel Cameron (047) pending. Helen Bias (050) pending. Both analytical — give the worked numbers.

certainty-seeker.best_framing: Do not offer until all uncertainties are resolved. Certainty-seekers will not commit while any question is open. Sam Corbett (052): mortgage question must resolve first.
certainty-seeker.notes: No successful offers to certainty-seekers in this dataset. Patience + information provision is the strategy.

EX-STL.best_timing: After documenting current provider failures. Make offer in the same meeting where GL-MGMT is confirmed.
EX-STL.notes: Highest-conversion profile type for offers. Switching EX-STL leads convert 43% of the time when offered.

STL-SW.best_timing: Only after property is confirmed vacant or delay is resolved. Do not offer to STL-SW leads with DLY-TEN or DLY-FUR.
STL-SW.notes: Offers to STL-SW before delays are resolved consistently stall.

PURCH.best_timing: After exchange (not before). Offer before exchange is premature — purchase may fall through.
PURCH.notes: Rebecca Keeling (018) has had offer for 60+ days with no conversion. Offer timing was pre-exchange.

SELL.best_timing: Only after sale falls through or lead confirms property is coming off market.
SELL.notes: All SELL leads with offers in this dataset either abandoned or chose sale.

ABROAD.best_timing: Before the lead departs. If they're leaving the country and haven't signed, set expiry date to match departure.
ABROAD.notes: Helen Bias (050) is the critical current case — departure deadline IS the offer deadline.

R2R.best_timing: Do not offer. Qualify R2R leads out before reaching offer stage.
R2R.notes: 0% acceptance across all R2R offers. Economics don't work.

7_days.notes: Too short for uncertain leads — creates pressure without conviction

14_days.notes: Optimal for ready leads (EX-STL, GL-MGMT present). Vanessa Wilson-Wood (036): 12 days to close.

21_28_days.notes: Better for leads with small remaining questions. Henry Roberts (045): 10 days from offer to close.

30_plus_days.notes: Extended deadlines lose urgency. Andrew McIntee (014): Aug 2026 expiry shows no urgency progression.
